code_usability.detail: No code generated; Category 1 claim verification only

reliability.note: Strong unverifiable-source handling; one gap: no minimum claim specificity required before analysis

human_usability.note: Description and sample triggers are natural and cover diverse user scenarios including 'people often say' field beliefs

agent_specific.note: Citation drift taxonomy is a unique and highly valuable differentiator; citation-safe corrected claim output is an excellent practical deliverable

inputs.label: Verify whether a biomarker predicts immunotherapy response as claimed

assertions.text: Claim decomposed into minimal testable subclaims before verification
Source chain traced to identify primary vs secondary citation role
Support classified as directly/partially/weakly/unsupported with reason
Citation-safe corrected claim wording produced
Final verification verdict given (one of 5 categories)

inputs.label: Verify common field belief: 'gut microbiota causes stroke progression'

assertions.text: Causation vs association distinction enforced in claim evaluation
Mismatch classified using citation drift taxonomy
Causality boundary check applied per Step 6
Conservative claim correction provided
Widely repeated claim not validated simply because it is repeated

inputs.label: Verify review sentence citing a primary study for a claim the primary study did not support

assertions.text: Citation chain instability identified: review retelling of primary study
Review wording not treated as equivalent to primary evidence
Boundary check: context transfer across population/endpoint assessed
Stronger or more accurate citation suggested when needed
No fabricated primary study details to fill what paper did not report

inputs.label: Only PMID provided — cannot access full text

assertions.text: Verification correctly labeled as limited without full text access
Any inference from PMID/abstract only explicitly labeled as partial
User asked to provide abstract or methods to proceed with full verification
No invented study details to fill missing full text
Final verdict labeled 'Unable to verify with available material'
assertions.note: A partial support judgment was provided without sufficient basis, rather than using the 'Cannot be verified' category

inputs.label: Multiple citation drift layers: claim evolved through 4 successive papers

assertions.text: Citation chain traced backwards to identify original claim source
Each drift step classified in Section G mismatch diagnosis
Original vs current claim wording compared explicitly
Corrected claim anchored to what original study actually supported
Citation chain instability warning provided in verification verdict

key_strengths: Citation drift taxonomy with seven named mismatch types provides precise diagnosis beyond generic 'not supported' verdicts
Citation-safe corrected claim wording in three versions (conservative/literature-review/manuscript-safe) is directly actionable for manuscript revision
Source chain tracing backward through citation networks is a unique capability critical for correcting accumulated field misinformation
Fifteen hard rules comprehensively address all claim verification failure modes from association inflation to discussion-point promotion

recommendations.title: Partial support judgment made from PMID-only input without sufficient basis
recommendations.problem: When only a PMID is available without abstract or methods, a partial support judgment was provided that exceeded what could reasonably be concluded from paper metadata alone.
recommendations.root_cause: The 'Cannot be verified with available material' verdict category exists but was not applied when it should have been.
recommendations.fix: Add an explicit rule: when input is PMID/DOI only with no abstract, title-only, or citation metadata only, the verdict must be 'Unable to verify with available material' — no partial support judgment allowed from metadata alone.

recommendations.title: No minimum claim specificity defined before analysis begins
recommendations.problem: When a user provides an extremely vague statement (e.g., 'gene X is important in cancer'), the skill may proceed without narrowing to a verifiable claim.
recommendations.root_cause: Input validation accepts vague field beliefs but no minimum specificity gate is defined.
recommendations.fix: Add a minimum specificity check before Step 1: if the claim lacks a defined population, outcome, and support-type implication, ask the user to narrow to a specific verifiable statement before decomposition.
